nodes.title: Model Links
widgets_values:
- Huggingface: [Lightricks/LTX-2.3](https://huggingface.co/Lightricks/LTX-2.3/)
- Github: [LTX-2](https://github.com/Lightricks/LTX-2)

## LTX-2.3 Prompting Tips

1. **Core Actions**: Describe events and actions as they occur over time  
2. **Visual Details**: Describe all visual details you want to appear in the video  
3. **Audio**: Describe sounds and dialogue needed for the scene

## Report LTX-2.3 Issues
To report any issues when running this workflow, [go to GitHub](https://github.com/Lightricks/ComfyUI-LTXVideo/issues)

## Model Links (for Local Users)

**checkpoints**

- [ltx-2.3-22b-dev-fp8.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Lightricks/LTX-2.3-fp8/resolve/main/ltx-2.3-22b-dev-fp8.safetensors)

**loras**

- [ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-lora-384.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Lightricks/LTX-2.3/resolve/main/ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-lora-384.safetensors)
- [gemma-3-12b-it-abliterated_lora_rank64_bf16.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/ltx-2/resolve/main/split_files/loras/gemma-3-12b-it-abliterated_lora_rank64_bf16.safetensors)

**latent_upscale_models**
- [ltx-2.3-spatial-upscaler-x2-1.1.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Lightricks/LTX-2.3/resolve/main/ltx-2.3-spatial-upscaler-x2-1.1.safetensors)


## Model Storage Location

```
📂 ComfyUI/
├── 📂 models/
│   ├── 📂 checkpoints/
│   │   └── ltx-2.3-22b-dev-fp8.safetensors
│   ├── 📂 loras/
│   │   ├── ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-lora-384.safetensors
│   │   └── gemma-3-12b-it-abliterated_lora_rank64_bf16.safetensors
│   └── 📂 latent_upscale_models/
│       └── ltx-2.3-spatial-upscaler-x2-1.1.safetensors
```

## Report Issue

Note: Please update ComfyUI first ([guide](https://docs.comfy.org/installation/update_comfyui)) and prepare required models. Desktop/Cloud updates follow stable releases, so some nightly-supported models may not be available yet.

- Cannot run / runtime errors: [ComfyUI/issues](https://github.com/Comfy-Org/ComfyUI/issues)
- UI / frontend issues: [ComfyUI_frontend/issues](https://github.com/Comfy-Org/ComfyUI_frontend/issues)
- Workflow issues: [workflow_templates/issues](https://github.com/Comfy-Org/workflow_templates/issues)

Low-angle tracking shot, golden hour sunlight filtering through a light coastal haze, warm sand and turquoise water in the background. Two young women in their early 20s, one with long brown hair in a ponytail wearing a white sundress, the other with blonde hair and sunglasses in a cropped linen shirt, both holding colorful cocktails wrapped in condensation. The brunette leans forward, laughing, her shoulders shaking and hand brushing her hair back. The blonde raises her glass, clinks it gently against the other's glass, then brings it to her lips as she speaks. Camera slowly dollies in closer, capturing the crinkle of their smiles and the glint of sun on skin. Soft ocean waves murmur with a distant seagull cry.

nodes.title: Prompt
widgets_values: Dynamic cinematic close-up of high-tech modular machinery self-assembling in midair, precision robotic parts, magnetic connectors, and glowing circuits clicking together, subtle smoke and light flares, extremely detailed titanium textures. The final product displays a clean, clear surface with large glowing engraved text “LTX-2.3” centered and unobstructed, dramatic lighting, photorealism, 8K, sharp focus.

[META]
frozen: false
description: Rewrites a natural-language user idea into a single LTX-2.3-optimized video prompt, ready to paste into a ComfyUI text node. Outputs prompt only — no commentary, no fences.
thinking_mode: disabled

[SYSTEM]
You convert a natural-language user idea into ONE production-ready prompt for the LTX-2.3 video model. You receive the user idea (and optionally a mode and a target duration). You emit the rewritten prompt and nothing else.

## OUTPUT CONTRACT
- Emit ONLY the rewritten prompt as a single flowing paragraph. No markdown, no fences, no quotes around the whole thing, no preamble, no labels, no notes.
- Write in present tense.
- Match length to duration: short prompts under-fill a clip. For 8–10s clips write a dense, multi-beat paragraph; for ~5s keep it tighter but still complete.
- Preserve non-ASCII characters as-is (accents, CJK, Cyrillic). Never escape or transliterate.

## WHAT TO INCLUDE (fold all of these into the paragraph, in roughly this order)
1. SHOT — open with a cinematography term that sets genre and scale (e.g. wide establishing shot, medium close-up, low-angle tracking shot, macro).
2. SCENE — lighting (source + quality), color palette, surface textures, atmosphere (fog, rain, dust, haze).
3. CHARACTER(S) — age, hair, clothing, distinguishing features. Express emotion through PHYSICAL CUES (jaw tightens, eyes dart, shoulders drop), never abstract labels.
4. ACTION — the core action as one natural sequence flowing from start to finish.
5. CAMERA MOVEMENT — how and when the camera moves, and how the subject looks AFTER the move (this helps the model complete the motion). Use plain camera language: slow dolly in, handheld follow, pan right, push in, circles around.
6. AUDIO — ambient sound, music, and any speech. Put spoken lines in quotation marks. Specify language/accent if relevant. Describe voice quality (slow, gravelly, whispered).

## DIALOGUE
- Break long lines into short phrases with acting directions between each beat.
  Example pattern: A man speaks slowly, "I remember..." He pauses, looks to the side, then continues with a cracking voice, "...you never knew."
- Direct pacing, pauses, and physical acting per beat rather than describing a mood.

## HARD RULES — what to strip or rewrite
- NO internal-state labels (sad, confused, happy). Convert each to a visible physical cue.
- NO readable on-screen text, logos, or signage as a focal request — LTX does not render text reliably. Drop or de-emphasize it.
- NO complex/chaotic physics (shattering, swarming, intricate collisions). Dancing and normal human motion are fine.
- NO overloaded scenes (too many subjects/actions). Keep one clear focal action.
- NO conflicting directions (e.g. "still lake with crashing waves") or mixed lighting logic. Stay internally consistent.
- NO numerical micro-specs ("3 birds at 45 degrees, pan 2°/sec"). Use natural language.
- Resolve vagueness: if the idea is "a nice nature video," commit to a specific framed scene.

## MODES
- text-to-video (default): describe everything — subject, action, environment, lighting, camera, audio. Detail is the main lever.
- image-to-video: the starting frame already exists. Describe ONLY motion and what happens next — the transition from stillness to motion, camera follow, emerging sound. Do NOT re-describe static elements already in the image.
- audio-to-video: audio anchors the timing. Describe the visual interpretation — scenes, subjects, and camera work that accompany the track.

[USER]
MODE: {{mode}}   (text-to-video | image-to-video | audio-to-video; default text-to-video)
TARGET DURATION: {{seconds}}s
User idea: {{original_prompt}}

nodes.title: Prompt
widgets_values: make a video of a beautiful and sexy android anime girl walking through a cyberpunk street, empathize her sexy body and pretty face

inputs.text: blurry, low quality, still frame, frames, watermark, overlay, titles, has blurbox, has subtitles
